claims.statement: The encryption state variable has 4 read sites: 0x1ddbc, 0x17b96, 0x17d4a, 0x18618.

claims.quote: 0x00    4     crypto_mode  uint32 LE, must be > 0 (e.g., 0x01000000)

claims.statement: Adapter validation at 0x1f798: payload length must be exactly 4 bytes (cmp r3, 4; bne 0x1f7d6 reject).

claims.quote: 0x1f7a4  ble 0x1f7d6           ; REJECT if value ≤ 0 (no response sent)

claims.statement: Rejection is silent: if payload is wrong size or value <= 0, the adapter exits without sending any response, and the host only knows via absence of an ack.
claims.quote: **Rejection is silent** — if payload is wrong size or value ≤ 0, the adapter exits without sending any response

claims.statement: The Command Injection via BoxSettings finding is rated CRITICAL and was Binary Verified in January 2026.
claims.quote: Command Injection via BoxSettings (CRITICAL) — Binary Verified Jan 2026

claims.statement: The firmware constructs shell commands using popen() and /bin/sh, inserting BoxSettings JSON values (wifiName, btName, oemIconLabel) directly into the command string without sanitization.
claims.quote: constructs shell commands using `popen()` and `/bin/sh`, inserting values from the BoxSettings JSON message

claims.statement: Shell metacharacters in the wifiName, btName, and oemIconLabel fields are interpreted by /bin/sh.
claims.quote: Shell metacharacters in these fields are interpreted by `/bin/sh`.

claims.statement: The wifiName field is inserted into the shell command `sed -i "s/^ssid=.*/ssid=%s/" /etc/hostapd.conf`.
claims.quote: sed -i "s/^ssid=.*/ssid=%s/" /etc/hostapd.conf

claims.statement: The btName field is inserted into the shell command `sed -i "s/name .*;/name \"%s\";/" /etc/bluetooth/hcid.conf`.
claims.quote: sed -i "s/name .*;/name \"%s\";/" /etc/bluetooth/hcid.conf

claims.statement: The oemIconLabel field is inserted into the shell command `sed -i "s/^.*oemIconLabel = .*/oemIconLabel = %s/" <target_file>`.
claims.quote: sed -i "s/^.*oemIconLabel = .*/oemIconLabel = %s/" <target_file>

claims.statement: The binary contains 50+ hardcoded shell command templates executed via popen().
claims.quote: The binary contains 50+ hardcoded shell command templates executed via `popen()`

claims.statement: The popen command template `sed -i "s/ssid=.*/ssid=%s/"` is triggered by the BoxSettings wifiName field and accepts user-controlled input.
claims.quote: `sed -i "s/ssid=.*/ssid=%s/"` | BoxSettings `wifiName` | Yes

claims.statement: The popen command template `sed -i "s/name .*/name \"%s\";"` is triggered by the BoxSettings btName field and accepts user-controlled input.
claims.quote: `sed -i "s/name .*/name \"%s\";"` | BoxSettings `btName` | Yes

claims.statement: The popen command template `killall AppleCarPlay; AppleCarPlay --width %d --height %d --fps %d` is triggered by the Open message and accepts integers only as input.
claims.quote: killall AppleCarPlay; AppleCarPlay --width %d --height %d --fps %d` | Open message | Integers only

claims.statement: The popen command template `tar -xvf %s -C /tmp` is triggered by the hwfs.tar.gz upload and accepts a path only as input.
claims.quote: `tar -xvf %s -C /tmp` | hwfs.tar.gz upload | Path only

claims.statement: The popen command template `/script/phone_link_deamon.sh %s start` is triggered by phone connection and uses hardcoded input.
claims.quote: `/script/phone_link_deamon.sh %s start` | Phone connection | Hardcoded

claims.statement: The popen command template `echo -n %s > /etc/box_product_type` is triggered by BoxSettings and possibly accepts user-controlled input.
claims.quote: `echo -n %s > /etc/box_product_type` | BoxSettings | Possibly

claims.statement: All firmware processes run as root, which is the only user on the system.
claims.quote: All firmware processes run as root (only user on system)

claims.statement: There is no sandboxing and processes have full filesystem access.
claims.quote: No sandboxing — full filesystem access

claims.statement: Injection in the wifiName or btName fields allows arbitrary command execution as root.
claims.quote: Injection in `wifiName`/`btName` fields allows arbitrary command execution as root

claims.statement: When injection is used the sed command fails so the SSID/name is not actually updated, requiring a second clean BoxSettings message to set the intended value.
claims.quote: When injection is used, the sed command fails, so the SSID/name is not actually updated; a second clean BoxSettings message is needed

claims.statement: Three command injection payloads were live-tested and confirmed on hardware via USB in January 2026, all executing as root.
claims.quote: Three payloads were live-tested and confirmed on hardware via USB. All executed as root.

claims.statement: Live command injection testing confirmed file creation, password change, and config modification; specific payloads are omitted from the document.
claims.quote: Specific payloads omitted from this document; results confirmed: file creation, password change, config modification.

claims.statement: A recommended mitigation for command injection is to sanitize all user inputs by stripping or escaping shell metacharacters before inserting into command strings.
claims.quote: Sanitize all user inputs — strip or escape shell metacharacters before inserting into command strings

claims.statement: A recommended mitigation is to replace popen() with execv()-family calls to avoid shell interpretation entirely.
claims.quote: Replace `popen()` with `execv()`-family calls to avoid shell interpretation entirely

claims.statement: A recommended mitigation is to whitelist allowable characters for name fields, limited to alphanumeric plus a limited set.
claims.quote: Whitelist allowable characters for name fields (alphanumeric + limited set)

claims.statement: A recommended mitigation is to run firmware processes as a non-root user where possible.
claims.quote: Run firmware processes as a non-root user where possible

claims.statement: A recommended mitigation is to enforce input length limits.
claims.quote: Enforce input length limits

claims.statement: The USB SessionToken key has the value W2EC1X1NbZ58TXtn and uses the AES-128-CBC algorithm.
claims.quote: SessionToken key | `W2EC1X1NbZ58TXtn` | AES-128-CBC

claims.statement: The USB Protocol payload key has the value SkBRDy3gmrw1ieH0 and uses the AES-128-CBC algorithm.
claims.quote: Protocol payload key | `SkBRDy3gmrw1ieH0` | AES-128-CBC

claims.statement: The USB encryption keys are embedded in the ARMadb-driver binary, visible after UPX unpacking.
claims.quote: Embedded in ARMadb-driver binary (visible after UPX unpacking)

claims.statement: All adapters share the same USB encryption keys.
claims.quote: All adapters share the same keys.

claims.statement: Both USB encryption keys have been identified across firmware versions, with differing context of use.
claims.quote: Both keys have been identified across firmware versions; context of use differs.

claims.statement: USB traffic can be decrypted by any party who has extracted the key from any unit.
claims.quote: USB traffic can be decrypted by any party who has extracted the key from any unit.

claims.statement: The A15W firmware image encryption key has the value AutoPlay9uPT4n17 and uses the AES-128-CBC algorithm.
claims.quote: Firmware image key | `AutoPlay9uPT4n17` | AES-128-CBC

claims.statement: The firmware encryption key is located in the ARMimg_maker binary via strings extraction.
claims.quote: ARMimg_maker binary (strings extraction)

claims.statement: Firmware images can be decrypted, modified, re-encrypted, and deployed using the known firmware key.
claims.quote: Firmware images can be decrypted, modified, re-encrypted, and deployed.

claims.statement: The firmware update process decrypts the .img file using the hardcoded key.
claims.quote: Decrypts `.img` using the hardcoded key

claims.statement: The firmware update process extracts tar.gz contents and executes once.sh from the extracted archive.
claims.quote:
Extracts tar.gz contents
3. Executes `once.sh` from the extracted archive

claims.statement: The firmware update process copies files to rootfs and performs no signature verification at any step.
claims.quote:
Copies files to rootfs
5. **No signature verification at any step**

claims.statement: Custom firmware can be created by decrypting official firmware, modifying contents, re-encrypting with the known key, and deploying via USB drive, SendFile (0x99), or OTA.
claims.quote: Custom firmware can be created by decrypting official firmware, modifying contents, re-encrypting with the known key, and deploying via USB drive, SendFile (0x99), or OTA.

claims.statement: The SendFile message uses message type 0x99 with a payload of 4-byte path length, N-byte null-terminated file path, 4-byte content length, and M-byte file content.
claims.quote:
Message Type: 0x99 (SendFile)
Payload:
  [4 bytes] path length
  [N bytes] file path (null-terminated)
  [4 bytes] content length
  [M bytes] file content

claims.statement: SendFile has no path traversal filter; there is no ../ sanitization.

claims.statement: SendFile has no file extension filter; any extension is accepted.
claims.quote: File extension filter | None | Any extension accepted

claims.quote: Size limit | Exists | Firmware logs `UPLOAD FILE Length Error!!!`; exact limit unknown

claims.quote: `/script/*` | Yes | Startup scripts

claims.quote: `/mnt/UPAN/*` | Yes | USB drive mount point
